topic: Llama 4 Scout Developer Guide 2026: 10M Token Context Window for Full Codebase Analysis

key_points: Scout and Maverick API access via multiple providers
OpenAI-compatible API setup with code examples
Pricing breakdown across DeepInfra, Groq, Together AI
Scout as low as $0.08/M input tokens
Production deployment guide for Scout and Maverick
Scout vs Maverick decision framework for indie devs
Hardware: single H100 GPU for Scout
10M context best for search/retrieval, not deep synthesis

stats.fact: Llama 4 Scout: 17B active parameters, 109B total, 16 MoE experts
10M token context covers approximately 2.5 million lines of code at ~4 tokens per line
Scout pricing: $0.08/M input and $0.30/M output via DeepInfra; $0.11/$0.34 via Groq
Groq Scout is 93% cheaper than Claude Haiku for batch classification and document summarization
Scout is 80x cheaper than Claude Opus 4.7 ($0.30/M vs $25/M output) for context-heavy tasks
Scout fits on a single NVIDIA H100 GPU with INT4 quantization at ~$1,440/month
Context window: Scout 10M vs Gemini 2.5 Pro 1M vs Claude Sonnet 4.5 200K tokens
Scout achieves perfect retrieval scores in long-context benchmarks up to 10.4M tokens
iRoPE applies NoPE layers every 4th layer for position-free global attention
Effective reliable context: ~5-6.5M tokens (advertised 10M) for consistent output quality

angles: The 10M context window as a paradigm shift: eliminate chunking and RAG for medium-sized codebases
Cost story: 80x cheaper than Claude Opus 4.7 and 93% cheaper than Claude Haiku for context-heavy workloads
Architecture deep dive: iRoPE enables 10M tokens while MoE keeps active compute at 17B params
Honest limits: effective context ~5-6.5M, synthesis quality drops beyond 2M — practical implications
Decision framework: Scout's massive context vs. RAG — when each approach wins
Provider comparison: Groq (speed), Together AI (reliability), DeepInfra (cheapest)
Self-hosting on a single H100: data privacy and cost predictability for teams
